documents.content:
TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION SHEET: CENTRIFUGAL PROCESS PUMP
Equipment Tag: PUMP-101A
Manufacturer: Flowserve Corp.
Standard: API 610 (Heavy Duty Refinery Service)

OPERATING LIMITS:
- Maximum Operating Pressure: 150 PSI (10.3 bar)
- Maximum Fluid Temperature: 110°C
- Bearing Housing Temperature Threshold: 85°C (185°F) - Critical Alarm Level
- Standard Vibration Warning Limit: 2.8 mm/s velocity (RMS) - Action Required
- Critical Vibration Trip Limit: 4.5 mm/s velocity (RMS) - Automatic Shutdown

TROUBLESHOOTING GUIDE (VIBRATION & BEARING TEMPERATURE):
1. Symptom: Bearing temperature > 80°C and rising.
   Possible Cause: Lubricant breakdown or shaft misalignment.
   Action: Verify oil levels. Inspect coupling alignment as detailed in Section 4.
2. Symptom: Shaft vibration velocity > 2.8 mm/s.
   Possible Cause: Rotor imbalance, bent shaft, or coupling misalignment.
   Action: Stop pump, perform dial indicator check on coupling shafts.

STANDARD OPERATING PROCEDURE (SOP)
Document Code: SOP-OPS-12
Title: Emergency Isolation & De-pressurization of High-Pressure Hydrocarbon Pumps
Target Equipment: PUMP-101A

PRE-MAINTENANCE ISOLATION CHECKLIST:
1. Verify Permit to Work (PTW) is signed by the Shift In-Charge.
2. Stop the driver motor. Turn off breaker at MCC Panel Room breaker #MCC-P101. Install lock and tag "DO NOT OPERATE - MAINTENANCE IN PROGRESS".
3. Close the suction and discharge block valves manually.
4. Locate and manually lock out electrical valve VALVE-102 (Discharge Isolation Control Valve). Apply padlocks.
5. Open the bleed valves to drain trapped hydrocarbon fluids to the closed blowdown system (Double Block & Bleed protocol).
6. Verify zero pressure on local gauge PG-101.
7. Conduct gas testing to ensure LEL (Lower Explosive Limit) is 0% before opening the pump housing.

REGULATORY NOTE:
All steps in this checklist comply strictly with the safety protocols of OISD-GDN-115 for hydrocarbon fluid systems.

OIL INDUSTRY SAFETY DIRECTORATE (OISD) STANDARD
Document Code: OISD-GDN-115
Title: Safe Start-up and Shut-down Procedures for Petrochemical Refineries

SECTION 5: HYDROCARBON PUMP ISOLATION MANDATES
5.1 General Requirements:
All centrifugal pumps handling flammable or toxic fluids above 60°C (including refinery unit feed pumps like PUMP-101A) must be equipped with a documented isolation checklist to prevent accidental release of hydrocarbons.

5.2 Isolation Configuration:
Double Block and Bleed (DBB) isolation systems are mandatory for lines exceeding 2 inches in diameter. The system must include two block valves with an intermediate drain/vent line to bleed trapped pressure safely.

5.3 Lockout/Tagout (LOTO):
Before line break or component replacement, electrical breakers must be locked out, and primary control valves (e.g., control valve VALVE-102) must be mechanically secured with padlocks.

5.4 Compliance Audits:
Plant managers must perform quarterly compliance audits on isolation checklists. Any SOP failing to reference DBB and LOTO protocols violates OISD-GDN-115 and represents a safety non-compliance.

INCIDENT FAILURE LOG
Incident ID: INC-2026-09
Date of Incident: June 14, 2026
Location: Crude Distillation Unit (CDU-1)
Equipment Affected: PUMP-101A (Refinery Feed Pump)

DESCRIPTION OF EVENT:
At 14:20 hrs, operators received a high-temperature alarm on PUMP-101A bearing housing, showing 88°C (exceeding the OEM thermal threshold limit of 85°C). Within 10 minutes, the temperature reached 92°C, accompanied by excessive pump vibration (registered at 4.2 mm/s, well past the 2.8 mm/s action limit). Before the automatic trip could trigger, a sudden failure occurred at the mechanical seal, causing a seal blowout and a localized hydrocarbon fluid leak. The emergency isolation SOP was initiated, block valves were closed, and electrical VALVE-102 was locked out. The unit was safely shut down.

ROOT CAUSE ANALYSIS (RCA):
Subsequent disassembly and inspection of the pump coupling revealed severe shaft misalignment. The coupling misalignment created cyclic fatigue loads on the rotating shaft, causing excessive vibration of 4.2 mm/s. The high vibration degraded the mechanical seal faces and generated friction heat, pushing bearing temperatures to 92°C and resulting in seal blowout. 

CORRECTIVE ACTIONS:
1. Re-align pump shaft using laser alignment system to under 0.05 mm tolerance.
2. Update local maintenance logs for PUMP-101A to require weekly vibration scans.
